title: hieracium murorum subsp. hepaticolor
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. hepaticolor, commonly known as murorum hawk's beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in treating liver and gallbladder issues. The health benefits of Hieracium murorum subsp. hepaticolor include improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function, making it a valuable remedy for individuals with liver or digestive problems. Hieracium murorum subsp. hepaticolor can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or decoction, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
